paragraphs.body: Title: Literature World Across The Atlantic Ocean Main point: The focus point between the library in the US and Taiwan is pretty much different, including the atmosphere in the space and the design of the building. Exposition: I’m using Carnegie Library in Pittsburgh as the place I’ll be writing about. I’ve never visited the library until I got to Pittsburgh last semester. Before I visited the Carnegie Library in Pittsburgh, I imagined it to be like an historical building which is covered by the atmosphere of art and humanism. I picture it to have crystal chandeliers and gorgeous decorations. (picture 1-1)

picture 1-1 picture 1-2 Conflict: By the time I finally visited Carnegie Library in Pittsburgh in person, I found the area inside the library really spacious and comfortable. Some of the parts look alike with the one in my imagination. Although it actually does have a historical and artful atmosphere, it doesn’t look that luxurious. The library has a cosy space where people are allowed to study or read. The book shelf and the study place are separated. In addition, a piano with earphones and a machine for composition are provided. (picture 1-2) Rising action: Conversely, the library near my home back in Taiwan is so diverse as the Carnegie Library in Pittsburgh. It won the name of Knowledge Canyon with the design of the building. Despite the fact that it is still in construction, we can see that it’s an environmentally friendly building with functions that will save energy through the blueprint of the library. It is planned to possess ventilation and the transmission of light, providing the readers a natural space to relax and read. (picture 1-3)

picture 1-3 picture 1-4 Climax: The Carnegie Library in Pittsburgh and the Knowledge Canyon in Hsinchu, Taiwan, are really different in either the appearance or the atmosphere. The former one focuses more on the inheritage of history and art; the latter dive more into how to utilize natural resources. (picture 1-4) Resolution: It is really interesting to seek the differences between the library in America and Taiwan. This experience helped me appreciate how Western libraries often preserve history and cultural identity in their design, creating a sense of continuity. On the other hand, the library in my hometown reflects a modern and progressive approach to architecture. It opened my eyes to how libraries in different cultures are not only spaces for learning but also symbols of different values for the society—whether it’s focusing on tradition or sustainability.

annotations.comment: 4 The outline is well-written! You may consider using your first language in the narrative to enrich its content. Overall, good job!
